mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and operational efficiency, which supports near-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of acceleration episodes, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation readings reduce real interest rate pressures and lower the probability of aggressive central bank tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of disinflation months, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness rather than seasonal adjustment, which depresses earnings outlooks and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when GDPC1 growth remains above 2% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and reduced business investment, which lowers near-term earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of contraction episodes, or the signal fails to predict direction when FEDFUNDS is below 2%.
